headline: Gender Identity Criminalised in All 14 State Shariah Enactments
context: All 14 Malaysian state and territory Shariah enactments criminalise 'men posing as women,' with 1,400 arrests of transgender individuals recorded between 2020 and 2025.

cards.big: 1,400 transgender Malaysians were arrested in 5 years.
cards.sub: The charge: existing in public as themselves. All 14 state Shariah laws criminalise gender non-conformity.

cards.big: Section 28 of the Shariah Criminal Offences Act (Federal Territories) targets 'male person posing as woman.'
cards.sub: Punishment: RM1,000 fine and 6 months imprisonment. No equivalent law targets women posing as men.

cards.big: Malaysia has an estimated 50,000 transgender individuals.
cards.sub: Employment discrimination forces 62% into informal or sex work — increasing vulnerability to violence.
No state has acted on the recommendation. Federal government deferred to state jurisdiction.

cards.big: Criminalising identity does not change identity. It criminalises people for being who they are.
The question is not whether Islam permits gender non-conformity — scholarly views vary. The question is whether the state should punish people for their identity. These are different questions.
